name: Hearty Burgundy Meatballs
recipeIngredient: 1 cup bread crumbs - Fresh, about 2 slices bread
8 ounces pearl onions - peeled and halved, about 1
1/2 cup red Burgundy - or other dry red wine
recipeInstructions:
1. In a large bowl, combine the beef, egg, bread crumbs, salt and pepper; using hands or a wooden spoon, blend well.
2. Shape the mixture into 1 ¼-inch balls.
3. In a 12-inch skillet over medium-high heat, heat the oil. Add the meatballs and cook about 12 minutes, turning frequently until well browned on all sides.
4. Using a slotted spoon, remove the meatballs to a plate. To the drippings in the skillet, add the carrots, mushrooms and onions; cook, still over medium-high heat, about 5 minutes, stirring frequently until crisp-tender.
5. Stir in the flour to coat the vegetables well; return the meatballs to the skillet, along with the wine, bouillon, 2 tablespoons parsley and 1 cup water.
6. Increase the heat to high; bring to a boil. Reduce the heat to low; simmer, covered, for 10 minutes until the meatballs are cooked through.
7. To serve: Spoon the meatballs and vegetables into a serving bowl; sprinkle with the remaining tablespoon of parsley. Serve accompanied by French bread.
